title: Local Only Integration Area
goal: Create a sanctioned local-only integration area for vendor-specific or runtime-specific bridges without turning it into shared workflow state.
Give local experimentation a safe boundary so ad hoc integrations stop leaking into repo-owned contracts.
non_goals: Do not create a shared plugin or extension framework in this lane.
Do not make local-only bridges part of checked-in required workflow state.
Do not widen the lane into runtime posture policy that belongs elsewhere.

intent.outcome: Define the local-only integration root, vendor/runtime subfolder convention, and boundary rules that allow disposable local bridges without contaminating shared repo contracts.
intent.constraints: The area must stay local-only, non-authoritative for shared workflow, easy to ignore by other contributors, and explicit about not carrying canonical planning or startup truth.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest root name and folder convention that is clear, low-residue, and compatible with existing local config and ignore rules.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the work starts requiring shared checked-in integration code, cross-vendor abstraction layers, or changes to canonical startup/planning authority.

execution.next_step: Choose the local-only root name and vendor/runtime subfolder convention, then codify the boundary and ignore rules in the smallest durable surfaces needed.
execution.proof: A local-only root and subfolder convention exist, boundary docs/config make its non-authoritative status explicit, and tests or checks prove shared surfaces do not start depending on it.

invariants: The integration area stays local-only and optional.
Shared repo contracts and startup surfaces do not depend on the area existing.
Vendor-specific bridges remain disposable and clearly outside canonical planning authority.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: create a sanctioned local-only integration area for vendor or runtime bridges without turning it into shared workflow state

required_continuation.activation trigger: continue until a local-only root and boundary rules exist and local experiments no longer need to piggyback on shared workflow state

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: planned: local integration shortcuts can gain a sanctioned home without expanding the shared product surface.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: runtime posture selection, broad plugin architecture, and any shared vendor abstraction work.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: explicit local-only boundaries can reduce pressure to misuse checked-in docs or config for disposable experiments, which in turn lowers trust confusion in the shared workflow.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: none yet; this is a prepared plan.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: prove that the chosen local-only root is discoverable enough for the local user but ignorable for everyone else, and that shared surfaces remain independent from it.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: land the root name, subfolder convention, and ignore/boundary rules first; only then consider any helper discovery or reporting.

intent_interpretation.literal request: prepare a full execplan for the fourth roadmap lane
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: shape the local-only integration lane into a safe, bounded planning artifact so later implementation does not accidentally turn local bridges into shared workflow authority
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: plan the lane around the smallest boundary-setting slice: root name, vendor/runtime subfolder convention, and explicit non-authority rules
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject work that introduces checked-in shared dependence on the local-only area or that treats vendor-specific bridges as canonical workflow surfaces

execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run agentic-workspace config --target . --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --format json; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "config or local or integration"; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before adding shared runtime adapters, plugin registries, or broad config-system changes beyond the local-only boundary needed for this lane
execution_bounds.stop before touching: packages/planning/ storage work, AGENTS.md startup routing, or runtime posture/handoff policy beyond local-only boundaries

stop_conditions.stop when: the lane would need a shared checked-in integration system rather than a local-only boundary and root convention
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: the chosen root cannot stay local-only without modifying canonical startup or planning authority
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: the work becomes vendor feature development instead of local-only boundary setting
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: config/doctor/tests cannot prove that shared surfaces remain independent from the local-only area

context_budget.live working set: local config override shape, ignore/boundary rules, local-only docs, focused config/doctor tests, lane issues
context_budget.recoverable later: older local-residue archive history and unrelated runtime posture work
context_budget.externalize before shift: the chosen root name, what belongs there, what must never live there, and how shared surfaces will ignore it
context_budget.pre-work config pull: agentic-workspace config --target . --format json; agentic-workspace doctor --format json
context_budget.tiny resumability note: keep this lane local-only and non-authoritative; root name and boundary rules come first
context_budget.context-shift triggers: after root naming, after ignore-rule decisions, or before any proposal to expose the area in shared startup surfaces

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: create a sanctioned but local-only home for disposable vendor/runtime bridges
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: the area is optional, local-only, non-authoritative, and must not become required shared workflow state
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: root name, vendor/runtime subfolder convention, and the minimum config/doc/test surfaces needed to prove the boundary
delegated_judgment.escalate when: a worker needs to introduce shared checked-in dependencies or broaden into a generic plugin architecture

references.label: choose local-only root and subfolder convention

active_milestone.scope: Pick the local-only root name, vendor/runtime subfolder convention, and explicit boundary rules that keep the area outside shared workflow authority.
active_milestone.optional_deps: gh issue refresh before implementation if #287/#288 wording changed materially

immediate_next_action: Decide the local-only root name and folder convention, then encode the non-authority boundary in the smallest config/doc/test surfaces required.
invariants: Local-only integration surfaces remain optional and disposable.
Shared repo workflow does not depend on the local-only area.
Vendor-specific bridges do not become canonical planning or startup truth.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: The local-only area must never become a required checked-in dependency for normal workflow.
Boundary docs and config should make the non-authority rule explicit from the first slice.
The first slice is only root and boundary setting, not bridge implementation.
open_questions_to_close: What root name is most explicit without adding cognitive residue to shared startup surfaces?
Should the local-only area be discoverable from config output, docs only, or a minimal optional report field?
Which ignore rule is safest: git ignore, local config only, or both?
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "config or local or integration"
completion_criteria: A local-only root and vendor/runtime subfolder convention are defined.
Boundary rules make clear that the area is optional and non-authoritative for shared workflow.
Config/doctor/tests prove shared surfaces remain independent from the local-only area.
The lane leaves a safe place for disposable local bridges without expanding the shared repo contract.

execution_run.next step: choose the root and boundary rules

finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: moderate until the local-only boundary is explicit enough that workers stop treating it as shared workflow state

proof_report.validation proof: planned: config/doctor queries, focused local-config tests, and contract/planning checks

intent_satisfaction.original intent: prepare a full execplan that safely bounds the local-only integration-area lane
intent_satisfaction.was original intent fully satisfied?: yes for planning preparation, no for lane implementation
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: the lane now has an explicit local-only boundary plan with path, validation, and non-authority constraints

closure_check.why this decision is honest: the planning artifact exists, but the lane's root convention and boundary proof do not
closure_check.evidence carried forward: this execplan plus the lane record in state.toml
closure_check.reopen trigger: revisit if local-only boundary work turns out to fit under an even narrower direct task instead

execution_summary.outcome delivered: prepared a full machine-first execplan for the local-only integration-area lane
